_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L9.5 'Royal-Grade Bridge' composited keeper and produce the L10 'Royal Champion' final variant.
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Royal Champion final = 5 visible adds (tall central GOLD crest between the horns rising from the helm apex with horns themselves growing taller and more sharply curved, GILT-FLANGED ORNATE MACE HEAD replacing the steel flanged head with buttery-gold gilt-flanged head with chased filigree on each flange and a small spiked finial at each flange tip plus gold haft end caps, HEAVY ORNATE 4-LAME PAULDRONS with sculpted gold-trimmed lion's-head motif on each pauldron's upper face, LONG FLARING TASSETS extending further down with each lame edged in gold piping and the LIVERY GONE — tassets are now plate + gold ornament only, ROYAL SHIELD EMBELLISHMENT raising and gilding the L9.5 cross emblem, replacing the central boss with a full gold dome with a ruby gem at center, and thickening the rim into a gold band with embossed gold studs). Single-image input — L1_Base scale anchor DROPPED at L10 to allow new vertical extent for the central crest. Silhouette mightiness rule peaks here: helm crest + 4-lame pauldrons + flaring tassets + larger shield embellishments give L10 a visibly mightier silhouette than L9.5.

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 detail-economy — REMOVED chased filigree along mace flanges (gold flange material change is the upgrade, NOT surface filigree); REMOVED per-flange-tip spiked finials (the gold-flanged read carries the upgrade without micro-spikes); REMOVED lion's-head emboss on pauldrons (sculpted figural emboss is on the canonical first-cut list — plain 4th lame is the volume upgrade); REMOVED per-lame gold piping on tassets (per-edge gold piping is the canonical first-cut item — flare + length + extended outward flare carry the L10 tasset upgrade); REMOVED embossed gold studs on the rim (clean wide gold rim band reads cleaner at thumbnail). L10 ornate read now comes from MATERIAL change (gold replaces steel on the mace head, shield boss, helm crest) and VOLUME (taller helm, broader 4-lame pauldrons, longer flaring tassets, larger shield) — NOT from sculpted motifs or per-edge piping. Updated CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION: L9.5 has NO gold knuckle-plates, NO cuirass/tasset edge piping, taller horns + gold spike caps.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L9_5/composite/infantry_L9_5_v2_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the 'Royal-Grade Bridge' kit accumulated on top of the existing base. You MUST preserve every prior feature shown in the input image: (a) the STEEL SALLET HELM dome, brim, brim rivets, gold band wrapping the helm at the brim line, closed visor with horizontal eye slit and breathing perforations — the FACE remains FULLY CONCEALED at this level (no skin / hair / eyes / nose / mustache / beard visible); (b) the existing UPWARD-CURVING STEEL HORNS rising from the helm temples — at the horns are already taller (~8-9 inches) and more sharply curved with GOLD POINTED SPIKE CAPS at their tips; at the horns reach final height and the gold caps remain at the tips; the new add is the central crest BETWEEN the horns per Edit Goal item 1; (c) the FULL STEEL CUIRASS — RAISED MEDIAL RIDGE plus ONE simple horizontal seam at the bottom-of-sternum line (NOT 3 articulated lames; engraved scrollwork was REMOVED at this level; per-edge gold piping was REMOVED at — the cuirass at has NO surface engraving and NO perimeter gold piping); (d) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle; (e) the 3-LAME STEEL PAULDRONS — preserved at this level; NO gold rivets at the seam (gold moved to a clear gold helm trim band), NO gold filigree on the upper face (filigree REMOVED at); the new step ADDS a fourth uppermost layer (PLAIN steel cap, NOT a sculpted lion's-head emboss) per Edit Goal item 3; (f) the STEEL VAMBRACES extending into STEEL GAUNTLETS with dark-iron knuckle-rivets (NO gold knuckle-plates — they were REMOVED at); (g) the FLANGED STEEL MACE HEAD with 6 vertical flanges held in the gauntleted anatomical-RIGHT hand — the new step REPLACES the steel head with a GILT-FLANGED ORNATE GOLD MACE HEAD per Edit Goal item 2; (h) the WOODEN MACE HAFT with leather grip cord on upper third and iron reinforcement strip running its length — the new step ADDS gold haft end caps per Edit Goal item 2 but the haft remains wood; (i) the LARGER STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with the embossed sun-burst boss + gold ring around boss base + GOLD CROSS EMBLEM inlaid on shield face + GOLD RIM BAND with gold rivet heads + four iron cross strap-bands radiating from boss — the new step RAISES + GILDS the cross emblem to be raised relief gold, REPLACES the boss dome with a full gold dome with a ruby gem at center, and THICKENS the rim into a wider gold band with embossed gold studs per Edit Goal item 5; (j) the STEEL SABATONS over warm-brown leather boots; (k) the LONGER 2-LAME STEEL TASSETS extending to mid-thigh — exactly 2 WIDER lames per tasset (NOT 3 narrow lames) with the existing PRONOUNCED OUTWARD FLARE; NO gold filigree, NO per-lame / per-edge gold piping (filigree removed at this level, per-edge piping removed at); the new step EXTENDS the tassets longer and FLARES them more outward AND REMOVES the livery skirt band per Edit Goal item 4 — NO new gold piping is added at either, the volume + flare carry the upgrade; (l) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH on the anatomical-RIGHT hip — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath; (m) the MAIL COIF back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, marking the FINAL Royal Champion tier. The unit gains its peak silhouette mightiness: helm extends UPWARD with a tall central gold crest (the silhouette envelope grows vertically), the mace head transitions from steel to fully gilt ornate gold, the pauldrons gain a fourth sculpted lame with lion's-head motif, the tassets flare longer with gold-edged lames and the LIVERY DISAPPEARS (full plate + gold ornament only), and the shield gains a ruby-gemmed gold boss + raised gilt cross + thickened gold rim. CRITICAL gold rendering note: every gold element MUST render as BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL with saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against cool silver-gray steel. Render gold with: saturated buttery-yellow base, soft top-down white-yellow highlight, deep honey-orange shadow on the underside. Each gold element must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint. (1) TALL CENTRAL GOLD CREST + TALLER HORNS — add a vertical fluted GOLD spike rising from the apex of the helm BETWEEN the two existing horns. The crest is approximately 8 inches tall, fluted (multiple vertical ridges running its length), tapering to a sharp point at the top. The crest is BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD, saturated warm yellow with the same gold language as the rest of the unit's gold accents. The crest's base sits on a small gold mount disc at the helm apex; the crest rises straight up between and slightly forward of the horns. ADDITIONALLY, the existing horns are now TALLER (~8 inches up from ~6 inches at this level) and MORE SHARPLY CURVED (more pronounced upward-and-back sweep). The horn bodies remain cool silver-gray steel; the gold spike caps at their tips remain gold. The helm-plus-crest-plus-horns silhouette now reads visibly TALLER than — silhouette mightiness rule active. (2) GILT-FLANGED ORNATE GOLD MACE HEAD + GOLD HAFT END CAP — REPLACE the cool silver-gray flanged steel mace head (with its 6 vertical flanges) with a gold-flanged mace head. The 6 vertical flanges remain (same shape and number); each flange's leading edge is now BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD with the recessed flange-channel staying cool silver-gray steel. The mace head reads as 'gold-flanged' at thumbnail (gold leading edges against steel channels, like alternating stripes) — distinctly different from the all-steel head. NO chased filigree along the flanges (filigree REMOVED 'rely on shapes/silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree'). NO per-flange-tip pointed spike finials; each flange ends in a clean rounded gold edge, NOT a pointed spike. ADDITIONALLY, the wooden haft remains warm-brown wood, but ONE GOLD END CAP is added at the top of the haft (where it meets the new gold-flanged mace head) — a short cylindrical gold band roughly 1 inch tall wrapping the haft just below the head. The leather grip cord on the upper third of the haft and the iron reinforcement strip remain unchanged. CRITICAL gold rendering: gold MUST render as buttery polished saturated YELLOW GOLD METAL with strong contrast against the cool silver-gray steel flange channels — saturated buttery-yellow base, soft white-yellow top-down highlight, deep honey-orange shadow on the underside. (3) HEAVY 4-LAME PAULDRONS — PLAIN STEEL FOURTH LAME, NO LION'S-HEAD EMBOSS — add a FOURTH uppermost layer to each pauldron, sitting ABOVE the existing small upper disc. The new uppermost lame is a smaller PLAIN cool silver-gray polished steel cap (~2 inches in diameter, slightly domed), with NO lion's-head emboss, NO figural motif, NO sculpted face, NO heraldic crest, NO gold trim, NO surface ornament beyond the simple lame-stack reads. The new uppermost cap extends slightly outward from the existing pauldron, broadening the shoulder silhouette. Each pauldron now reads as 4 stacked steel layers: the new plain steel uppermost cap + the existing small upper disc + the base disc visible in the input image + the existing downward bicep lame. The shoulder envelope is visibly BROADER than at this level — silhouette mightiness rule active (peak shoulder width). The volume + the lame-stack count is the upgrade; the surface is plain steel. (4) LONG FLARING TASSETS — PLAIN STEEL, LIVERY REMOVED — extend each existing 2-lame tasset DOWNWARD beyond mid-thigh (each tasset now ~12 inches tall, up from ~9 inches at the prior level). The tassets FLARE outward more strongly at the lower edge — the bottom corners are now visibly wider than the top corners with a clear pronounced flare at the lower edge of each tasset, exaggerating the existing outward flare. The 2 wider lames per tasset remain (NOT 3, NOT a return to thin multi-lame fragmentation). NO gold piping along the lame edges, NO gold perimeter trim line, NO gold tasset edges — the lames are PLAIN cool silver-gray steel; the volume + flare + length carry the upgrade. ADDITIONALLY, the LIVERY skirt band visible through the front gap between the two tassets at this level / is now FULLY REMOVED — there is NO red+mustard cloth visible at the front of the body at this level. The narrow gap between the two tassets now shows either: (a) the steel inner edges of the tassets touching at the top with the gap narrowing to almost nothing, OR (b) a small visible portion of dark blue trousers in the gap. The character is now full plate with NO cloth livery visible at the torso or hip area. The dark blue trousers remain visible at the calf below the lower edge of the longer tassets. (5) ROYAL SHIELD EMBELLISHMENT — RUBY-GEMMED GOLD BOSS + RAISED GILT CROSS + CLEAN WIDE GOLD RIM — RAISE and GILD the existing inlaid gold cross emblem on the shield face: the cross is now RAISED RELIEF (stands proud of the shield face by approximately ¼ inch), fully gilded buttery polished gold, four equal arms, each arm now slightly thicker. The cross arms still stop short of the iron cross strap-bands. REPLACE the central boss with a FULL GOLD DOME — the cool silver-gray steel boss dome from the levels above is now entirely buttery polished gold, slightly larger and more pronounced (~4 inches diameter, up from 3.5 inches). At the apex of the gold dome, add a single SMALL ROUND RUBY GEM — saturated red glass-gem with bright catchlight, approximately ¾ inch diameter, set in the center of the gold boss dome. The sun-burst rays radiating from the boss dome become gold (matching the new gold dome) — they are now raised gold rays, not steel. ADDITIONALLY, THICKEN the existing gold rim band into a CLEAN WIDE GOLD BAND — the rim is now approximately 1 inch wide (up from ~½ inch at this level), a smooth uniform gold band wrapping the entire perimeter. NO embossed gold studs / gold rivet heads / per-stud ornament along the rim; the rim reads as one clean wide gold band at thumbnail. The four iron cross strap-bands remain dark cool-gray iron at this level — they are NOT gilded; the gold accents at are: clean wide gold rim band, raised gold cross emblem, gold boss dome with sun-burst rays, ruby gem. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: the helm dome, brim, brim rivets, gold helm circumference band; the closed visor with eye slit and breathing perforations; the cuirass with its raised medial ridge plus ONE simple horizontal seam (NOT 3 lames — reduced, NO tonal scrollwork engraving (removed at), NO gold edge piping (removed at); the wide leather belt with iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath; the steel vambraces extending into steel gauntlets with dark-iron knuckle-rivets (NO gold knuckle-plates — removed at); the leather grip cord on the upper haft; the iron reinforcement strip on the wooden haft; the wooden mace haft (haft remains wood; mace head replaced per Edit Goal item 2; gold end caps added); the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers visible at the calf; the steel sabatons over warm-brown leather boots; the mail coif back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm; the round shield shape (only the rim, boss, cross, and rays change at this level). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. Mace arm extended forward and slightly down, gauntleted hand grips upper haft, gold gilt-flanged mace head dangling at hip height. Shield arm bent at elbow, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height. Calm guard stance. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. The new central crest + taller horns extend the silhouette UPWARD — that vertical growth is allowed and intentional at this level. DO NOT crop the crest or horns at the top of the canvas — if needed, the model may rescale the figure slightly to keep all features within the canvas. DO NOT shrink the figure aggressively. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT keep the steel mace head — the mace head MUST be fully gilt buttery gold at this level; DO NOT make the gold mace head dim, brass-toned, bronze-toned, or yellow-painted — it must read as saturated polished YELLOW GOLD METAL; DO NOT replace the wooden haft with metal — haft remains wood with gold end caps only; DO NOT remove the leather grip cord or the iron reinforcement strip on the haft; DO NOT add spikes / pointed finials at each flange tip on the gold-flanged mace head — per-flange-tip spiked finials were REMOVED 2026-05-07 'chased filigree on weapon flanges / spikes / banding rings'; each flange ends in a clean rounded gold edge with NO spike, NO pointed finial, NO micro-spike; DO NOT add chased filigree / scrollwork / leaf-and-curl etching along the mace flanges — chased filigree was REMOVED 2026-05-07; the gold-flanged read (gold leading edges + cool steel flange channels) is the upgrade with NO surface decoration; DO NOT make the central crest a feather plume, peacock plume, ostrich feather, or any non-metal crest — the crest is a SOLID GOLD FLUTED SPIKE; DO NOT make the crest curve to either side — it rises STRAIGHT UP from the helm apex; DO NOT shrink the horns at this level — they grow TALLER and more sharply curved; DO NOT make the horns gold — only the spike caps at the tips are gold (from.5); horn bodies remain steel; DO NOT replace the closed visor with an open helm — face remains FULLY CONCEALED; DO NOT show any skin, hair, eyes (other than the slit), nose, mustache, or beard at this level; DO NOT add a 5th lame to the pauldrons — exactly 4 stacked layers at this level; DO NOT add a sculpted lion's-head emboss / lion's face / lion's mane / heraldic beast motif / figural emboss on the new uppermost pauldron lame — sculpted lion's-head emboss was REMOVED 2026-05-07 ('sculpted figural emboss / lion's-head' is on the canonical first-cut list); the new uppermost lame is a PLAIN cool silver-gray polished steel cap with NO surface ornament; DO NOT add gold trim, gold filigree, or gold edge piping on the 4th pauldron lame — the volume bump (the 4th lame existing) IS the upgrade; the lame is plain steel; DO NOT add per-lame gold edge piping / gold trim line / gold perimeter on the tassets — per-edge gold piping was REMOVED 2026-05-07 ('per-edge gold piping is the canonical first-cut item'); each tasset lame is plain steel with NO gold piping at its edges; DO NOT make each tasset more than 2 wider lames — exactly 2 wider rectangular lames per tasset at this level (NO return to thin multi-lame fragmentation); DO NOT extend the tassets beyond ~12 inches; DO NOT make the tassets one continuous skirt — they remain TWO SEPARATE plates with a vertical gap at the front; DO NOT add embossed gold studs / per-stud ornament / gold rivet heads along the shield rim — embossed gold studs on the rim were REMOVED 2026-05-07 ('embossed studs on shield rim, use a clean wider band instead'); the rim is a CLEAN WIDE GOLD BAND with NO studs and NO per-rivet ornament; DO NOT add gold knuckle-plates on the gauntlets at this level — per-knuckle gold plates were REMOVED at this level; the gauntlet knuckles remain dark-iron rivets; DO NOT add gold edge piping on the cuirass — per-edge gold piping on the cuirass was REMOVED at this level; the cuirass perimeter is plain steel with NO gold trim line; DO NOT keep any red+mustard livery skirt visible at this level — the livery is FULLY GONE at this level (no cloth visible at the torso or hip area); DO NOT add new cloth garments at the hip or torso to replace the removed livery; DO NOT change the trouser color (trousers remain dark blue / muted steel-blue, visible at the calf); DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT make the central ruby gem any color other than saturated red — NOT blue, NOT green, NOT purple, NOT clear; DO NOT make the ruby gem larger than ~¾ inch — it is a small gem at the center of the gold dome; DO NOT make the gold boss dome the entire shield face — only the central boss area becomes a gold dome; DO NOT gild the four iron cross strap-bands — they remain dark cool-gray iron at this level; DO NOT make the gold cross emblem flat-inlaid at this level — it is RAISED RELIEF at this level (stands proud of the shield face); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing direction (still three-quarter view); DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
